Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.title: Pepper Bell Bacterial Spot
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.cause: This disease is caused by bacterial infection and spreads rapidly in warm and wet conditions.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Dark brown spots appear on leaves and fruits. Leaves may turn yellow and dry.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply copper-based bactericide.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.fertilizer: Use balanced NPK fertilizer with compost.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Avoid overhead watering and use disease-free seeds.

Pepper__bell___healthy.title: Healthy Pepper Leaf
Pepper__bell___healthy.fertilizer: Use organic compost and balanced fertilizer.
Pepper__bell___healthy.prevention: Maintain regular watering and proper sunlight.

Potato___Early_blight.title: Potato Early Blight
Potato___Early_blight.cause: Caused by Alternaria fungus during warm and humid weather.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown circular spots with yellow edges appear on leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove infected leaves and spray fungicide.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Avoid excess moisture and rotate crops.

Potato___healthy.title: Healthy Potato Leaf
Potato___healthy.prevention: Inspect crop regularly and maintain clean field.

Potato___Late_blight.title: Potato Late Blight
Potato___Late_blight.cause: Caused by Phytophthora infestans fungus in wet conditions.
Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked spots appear on leaves and stems.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants and spray fungicide.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Avoid waterlogging and improve air circulation.

Tomato___Target_Spot.title: Tomato Target Spot
Tomato___Target_Spot.cause: Fungal infection caused by Corynespora cassiicola.
Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Circular brown target-like spots appear on leaves.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Use recommended fungicide and remove infected leaves.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Keep field dry and improve ventilation.

Tomato_Tomato_mosaic_virus.title: Tomato Mosaic Virus
Tomato_Tomato_mosaic_virus.cause: Viral infection spread by contaminated tools and insects.
Tomato_Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Leaves become mottled, curled, and distorted.
Tomato_Tomato_mosaic_virus.fertilizer: Use organic fertilizer for stronger immunity.
Tomato_Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Disinfect tools and control insect vectors.

Tomato_Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.title: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato_Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.symptoms: Leaves curl upward and turn yellow.
Tomato_Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.treatment: Remove infected plants and control whiteflies.
Tomato_Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.prevention: Use insect nets and resistant varieties.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.title: Tomato Bacterial Spot
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.cause: Bacterial infection during wet and humid weather.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark spots appear on leaves and fruits.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.treatment: Spray copper bactericide and remove infected leaves.

Tomato_Early_blight.title: Tomato Early Blight
Tomato_Early_blight.cause: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani.
Tomato_Early_blight.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings appear on leaves.
Tomato_Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicide and remove infected leaves.
Tomato_Early_blight.fertilizer: Use calcium and balanced NPK fertilizer.
Tomato_Early_blight.prevention: Avoid wet leaves and rotate crops.

Tomato_Late_blight.title: Tomato Late Blight
Tomato_Late_blight.cause: Caused by fungal infection during cool and wet weather.
Tomato_Late_blight.symptoms: Large dark patches appear on leaves and stems.
Tomato_Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants and use fungicide.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.title: Tomato Leaf Mold
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.cause: Fungal disease caused by excess humidity.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Yellow spots form on upper leaf surface.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.title: Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.cause: Caused by Septoria fungus in wet weather.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with gray centers appear.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and spray fungicide.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.title: Tomato Spider Mite
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.cause: Spider mites attack plants during hot and dry weather.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.symptoms: Tiny yellow spots and webbing appear on leaves.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.fertilizer: Use organic compost for plant strength.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.prevention: Keep plants hydrated and inspect leaves regularly.
